name: Deere & Company
summary: Deere & Company (DE, Industrials) — composite stance BULLISH. Latest reported quarter FY2026 Q1: revenue $8.0B (+17.5% YoY), net margin 8.2%. Management credibility 1.00 on 9 claims. Valuation 16.5x forward P/E (+32% vs peers). Spot $529.15 (above 200-DMA).

bull: Top-line momentum — revenue grew +17.5% YoY in FY2026 Q1 to $8.0B, with TTM revenue of $36.34B confirming the run-rate. Growth at this pace is unusual for a company of this scale and reflects either share gains, pricing power, or new-product cycles depending on the line-item mix in the Fundamental tab.
Margin discipline — 8.2% net margin holds the company in the upper half of its peer set. Operating-cost growth continues to lag revenue growth — the classic margin-expansion setup.

Management credibility — MCS of 1.00 on 9 tracked forward claims (5B/0H/0M record). When management says they will deliver, they typically do — a meaningful re-rating support that is often overlooked in quantitative screens.

Range-positioning balanced — at 40% of the 52-week range ($439.11–$662.49), the setup is neither at a momentum-exhaustion ceiling nor at a capitulation floor. Risk/reward into the next catalyst window screens reasonable.
bear: Margin trajectory deteriorating — net margin compressed -9.2pp over the trailing quarters. This typically precedes a guidance reset by 1–2 quarters as management gets ahead of the cost-curve issue.

Valuation premium — 16.5x forward P/E vs peer median of 12.5x — a 32% premium. The setup requires continued out-execution to maintain; any reversion to peer-median multiples compresses the stock meaningfully even before any earnings revision.
trigger_up: Revenue growth re-accelerates above 20% YoY on next print — confirms the demand environment hasn't degraded into the back half.
Net margin expands above 9.7% — confirms pricing power and/or cost discipline thesis.
Management raises FY guide on next quarterly call — direct upward revision to consensus.
trigger_down: Revenue growth decelerates below 14% YoY — signals demand has materially softened versus the latest disclosed run-rate.
Net margin compresses below 6.2% — flags either input-cost pressure or competitive dynamics shifting unfavorably.
Management cuts FY guide on next quarterly call — direct downward revision; historically sees 5–15% drawdown on print.
Decisive break below the 52-week low at $439.11 — technical confirmation that the long-term trend has rolled over.
bottom_line:
BULLISH. Deere & Company (DE) printed $8.0B in revenue in FY2026 Q1 (+17.5% YoY), net margin of 8.2%. Management credibility is strong (MCS 1.00, 5B/0H/0M record), supporting take-up of the forward guide. At 16.5x forward P/E, the stock carries a 32% premium to peers (12.5x median) — the multiple needs continued execution to defend.

Sell-side consensus PT of $520 implies -1.7% upside. 30-day implied move is ±7.6%. Position-sizing should reflect conviction; use the trigger list to manage adds and trims around the next earnings catalyst.
